titleStmt.title: Letter from J. T. Roche to the Under-Secretary for Ireland, 15 April 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: J. T. Roche

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: A letter from J. T. Roche, Secretary to Westmeath County Council, to Sir Matthew Nathan (1862-1939), Under Secretary for Ireland, requesting Westmeath County Council be reimbursed for court expenses for the half-year to 31 March 1916.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers; CSO RP/1916/5620/6732

correspAction.placeName: Council Chambers, Mullingar, County Westmeath, Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Westmeath County Council
Council Chambers Mullingar 15th April, 1916
Encl. Sir, I beg to enclose Schedule of payments made in the
half—year ended 31st March 1916 together with the
necessary Vouchers, in connection with advances to
Prosecutors and Witnesses for expenses at Assizes Quarter
and Petty Sessions amounting to £70.5.1 in respect
of which recoupment is claimed by my Council. I am Sir,
Your Obedient Servant, J. T. Roche The Under Secretary
Dublin Castle,
DUBLIN.
